feat\(scanner\): Sprint 44 Day 1-4 - SDLC Structure Scanner Engine

Implemented core SDLC Structure Scanner with 4 validators and 12 validation rules.

**Sprint 44 Progress**: 58.1% complete \(4,937/8,500 lines\)
**Rules**: 12/15 complete \(80%\)
**Validators**: 4/5 complete \(80%\)

## Day 1-2: Foundation & Configuration \(2,754 lines\)

### Core Architecture \(Day 1: 1,789 lines\)
- ViolationReport dataclass with severity levels \(ERROR, WARNING, INFO\)
- BaseValidator abstract class with plugin pattern
- ValidatorRegistry for auto-registration
- SDLCStructureScanner with parallel execution \(ThreadPoolExecutor\)
- Comprehensive unit tests \(55+ cases, 93%+ coverage\)

### Configuration System \(Day 2: 965 lines\)
- ScannerConfig with rule customization
- ConfigLoader with hierarchical search
- Rule-level override \(enabled, severity, auto_fix\)
- Path ignore patterns
- .sdlc-config.example.json template

## Day 3-4: Validators Implementation \(2,183 lines\)

### Stage & Numbering Validators \(Day 3: 1,339 lines\)
- **StageFolderValidator**: STAGE-001 to STAGE-005 \(5 rules\)
  - Invalid naming, unknown stages, name mismatch, duplicates, missing stages
- **SequentialNumberingValidator**: NUM-001 to NUM-003 \(3 rules\)
  - Duplicate numbering, sequence gaps, invalid format

### Naming & Header Validators \(Day 4: 844 lines\)
- **NamingConventionValidator**: NAME-001 to NAME-002 \(2 rules\)
  - Invalid characters, inconsistent casing \(kebab-case enforcement\)
- **HeaderMetadataValidator**: HDR-001 to HDR-002 \(2 rules\)
  - Missing headers, invalid format \(YAML frontmatter support\)

## Key Features

**Auto-Fix Support**: 9/12 rules \(75%\)
- STAGE-001, STAGE-003, STAGE-005
- NUM-002, NUM-003
- NAME-001, NAME-002
- HDR-001, HDR-002

**Performance**: 
- Parallel validation \(4 workers default\)
- Target: <30s for 1K files
- Configurable via .sdlc-config.json

**Integration**:
- Plugin pattern \(auto-registration\)
- Multiple output formats \(JSON, text, GitHub Actions\)
- Comprehensive test coverage \(90%+ across all validators\)

## Files Changed

**New Implementation** \(10 files, 2,919 lines\):
- validation/violation.py \(201 lines\)
- validation/base_validator.py \(273 lines\)
- validation/structure_scanner.py \(457 lines\)
- validation/config.py \(423 lines\)
- validation/validators/stage_folder.py \(348 lines\)
- validation/validators/sequential_numbering.py \(306 lines\)

- validation/validators/header_metadata.py \(347 lines\)
- validation/validators/__init__.py \(29 lines\)
- .sdlc-config.example.json \(61 lines\)

**New Tests** \(7 files, 1,971 lines\):
- test_violation.py \(494 lines\)
- test_base_validator.py \(402 lines\)
- test_config.py \(402 lines\)
- test_stage_folder_validator.py \(301 lines\)
- test_sequential_numbering_validator.py \(360 lines\)
- test_naming_convention_validator.py \(80 lines\)
- test_header_metadata_validator.py \(75 lines\)

**Design Documentation** \(4 files\):
- Scanner-Architecture-Design.md \(~500 lines\)
- Validator-Rules-Specification.md \(~450 lines\)
- SPRINT-44-KICKOFF-APPROVAL.md \(758 lines\)
- SPRINT-44-READINESS-ASSESSMENT.md \(330 lines\)

**Updated**:
- validation/__init__.py \(+27 lines - exports\)

## Quality Metrics

- **Test Coverage**: 92%+ average across all components
- **Test Cases**: 105+ total
- **Auto-Fix Coverage**: 75% \(9/12 rules\)
- **Performance**: Parallel execution ready
- **Documentation**: Elite+ \(comprehensive docs + tests\)

## Next Steps \(Day 5-10\)

feat\(scanner\): Sprint 44 Day 5 - CrossReferenceValidator

Implement CrossReferenceValidator with 3 rules for SDLC 5.1.1 compliance:

## Rules Implemented

1. **REF-001**: Broken internal links \(ERROR\)
   - Detect markdown links to non-existent files
   - Skip external links \(http, https, mailto\)
   - Handle anchors in links \(file.md#section\)
   - Relative path resolution \(../, ./\)

2. **REF-002**: Orphaned files \(WARNING\)
   - Detect files not referenced by any document
   - Skip README.md, index.md \(allowed unreferenced\)
   - Only flag .md, .txt, .rst, .adoc files

3. **SCANNER-001**: Structure health metrics \(INFO/WARNING\)
   - Calculate broken link percentage
   - Calculate orphaned file percentage
   - Determine health status \(HEALTHY, NEEDS ATTENTION, UNHEALTHY\)
   - Reference density metrics

## Files Changed

**New Implementation** \(376 lines\):
- backend/sdlcctl/validation/validators/cross_reference.py
  - CrossReferenceValidator class
  - Markdown link extraction \(regex-based\)
  - Reference graph building
  - Health metrics calculation

**New Tests** \(500 lines\):
- backend/tests/unit/validation/test_cross_reference_validator.py
  - 22 test cases covering all rules
  - Edge cases: Unicode, hidden files, node_modules
  - Complex reference graphs

**Updated** \(4 lines\):
- backend/sdlcctl/validation/validators/__init__.py
  - Auto-register CrossReferenceValidator

**Day 5 Delivered**: 876 lines \(~900 target\)
**Total Sprint Progress**: 5,813/8,500 lines \(68.4%\)
**Rules Complete**: 15/15 \(100%\) 🎉

## All 15 Rules Complete

## Summary
CEO directive to standardize on single frontend platform \(Next.js\).
ADR-024 superseded. Migration planned for Sprint 61-64.

## Documents Created/Updated
- ADR-025: Frontend Platform Consolidation \(NEW\)
- ADR-024: Updated status to SUPERSEDED
- SPRINT-61-64-FRONTEND-PLATFORM-CONSOLIDATION.md \(NEW\)

- CURRENT-SPRINT.md: Updated with new priorities
- CTO-DECISION-AGENTSCOPE-ANALYSIS.md: Sprint refs updated

## Migration Strategy
- Strangler pattern \(no big-bang rewrite\)
- Phase 0: Spike + go/no-go \(3-5 days\)
- Phase 1-2: Route group migration \(4-5 weeks\)
- Phase 3: Cutover + deprecate Vite \(1 week\)

## Key Decisions
- Platform: Next.js 14+ \(App Router\)
- Dashboard: Client Components for SPA-like UX
- Performance budget: Navigation <100ms, Bundle <1MB

chore\(OpenCode\): ABORT evaluation - Refocus on Track 1 SASE \(Q1 2026 P0\)

DECISION: Abort OpenCode evaluation after 4 hours \(Jan 12, 2026\)
REASON: Strategic misalignment + resource prioritization
BUDGET: $90K saved → Reallocated to Vibecode CLI + SASE

## Why Aborted

1. **Strategic Misalignment**:
   - OpenCode is CLI/TUI tool \(competitor to Claude Code, Cursor\)
   - No HTTP API for integration \(ADR-026 assumption invalid\)
   - Would require $50K-$80K fork + custom API layer
   - Better positioning: Govern ALL AI coders \(not integrate one\)

2. **Resource Prioritization**:
   - Track 1 SASE is Q1 2026 P0 \(requires full team focus\)
   - SDLC 5.1.0 Framework Enhancement \(Weeks 1-14, Dec 9 - Apr 11\)
   - $90K better spent on Vibecode CLI \(our differentiator\)

## What We Learned \(4 hours\)

After cloning /home/nqh/shared/opencode:
- OpenCode = TypeScript/Bun CLI application \(Interactive TUI\)
- No REST API endpoints exist
- Cannot integrate via adapter pattern
- Better strategy: Governance layer ABOVE all AI coders

## Changes

### Archived \(docs/99-archive/OpenCode-Evaluation-Aborted-Jan12-2026/\)
- ADR-026-OpenCode-Integration-Strategy.md
- ISSUE-OpenCode-Level0-Week1-2.md
- OpenCode-Critical-Discovery-Jan12-2026.md
- OpenCode-Setup-Instructions-ACTUAL.md
- OpenCode-Quick-Start-Guide.md
- OpenCode-Deliverables-Summary.md
- OpenCode-CTO-Email-Draft.md
- OpenCode-Slack-Message-Draft.md
- README-ABORT-DECISION.md \(new\)
- SUMMARY.md \(new\)

### Updated
- CURRENT-SPRINT.md: Removed OpenCode section, refocused on SASE
- Product-Roadmap.md: Removed OpenCode L0-L3, added Vibecode CLI \($30K Q2\)

## Budget Reallocation \($90K\)

- Level 1 \($30K\) → Vibecode CLI enhancements \(Q2 2026\)
- Level 2 \($20K\) → SASE artifact development
- Level 3 \($40K\) → Vibecode CLI optimization \(H2 2026\)

## Next Focus \(Q1 2026\)

1. Track 1 SASE \(P0\) - SDLC 5.1.0 Framework
2. Vibecode CLI planning \($90K reallocated\)
3. EP-06 IR Codegen \(Sprint 45-50\)

---

Timeline: Started 9am Jan 12 → Discovered reality 9:30pm → Aborted 10:15pm
Total Investment: 4 hours \(better than $90K + 12 weeks\)
Key Takeaway: Validate architecture assumptions BEFORE committing

Related: Track 1 SASE Week 5 \(Jan 13-17, 2026\)
Status: ❌ OpenCode ABORTED, ✅ SASE REFOCUSED
EOF
\)")

Sprint 108 Day 2: Auto-Generation Layer COMPLETE \(GREEN Phase\)

## Auto-Generation Service \(FIX 1: Make compliance automatic\)
- 4 generators with 3-level fallback \(LLM → Template → Minimal\)
- Principle: "GOVERNANCE MUST BE THE FASTEST WAY" \(<5 min per PR\)
- Fail-safe: Auto-generation failure never blocks governance

### Generators Implemented:
1. IntentGenerator: Task → Intent document \(<10s, ~15 min saved\)
2. OwnershipGenerator: File → @owner annotation \(<2s, ~2 min saved\)
3. ContextAttachmentGenerator: PR → ADR/spec links \(<5s, ~5 min saved\)
4. AttestationGenerator: AI session → Attestation template \(<3s, ~8 min saved\)

### Files Created:
- backend/app/config/auto_generation.yaml \(439 LOC\)
- backend/app/config/governance_signals.yaml \(Vibecoding Index weights\)
- backend/app/config/critical_paths.yaml \(Security/payment paths\)
- backend/app/config/feedback_templates.yaml \(5 actionable templates\)
- backend/app/config/break_glass.yaml \(Emergency bypass\)
- backend/app/config/governance_flags.py \(Feature flags\)
- backend/app/services/governance/auto_generator.py \(1,233 LOC\)
- backend/app/api/routes/auto_generation.py \(703 LOC\)
- backend/tests/services/governance/test_auto_generator.py \(692 LOC\)

### API Endpoints:
- POST /api/v1/auto-generate/intent
- POST /api/v1/auto-generate/ownership
- POST /api/v1/auto-generate/context
- POST /api/v1/auto-generate/attestation
- POST /api/v1/auto-generate/all
- GET /api/v1/auto-generate/health

## Metrics:
- Total Day 2: ~3,500 LOC
- Target: Reduce compliance from 30 min → <5 min per PR

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

Bash(git commit -m "$\(cat <<''EOF''
Sprint 108 Day 3: Governance Mode & Dogfooding COMPLETE \(GREEN Phase\)

## Governance Mode Service \(WARNING/SOFT/FULL Enforcement\)
- 4 enforcement levels: OFF, WARNING, SOFT, FULL
- Auto-rollback on high rejection/false positive rate
- Kill switch for emergency rollback
- Metrics tracking for calibration
- Project-level mode overrides

### Mode Progression:
- OFF: No enforcement \(development mode\)
- WARNING: Log only, no blocking \(observability\)
- SOFT: Block CRITICAL/ERROR, warn others
- FULL: Block all violations \(production\)

### Files Created:
- backend/app/services/governance/mode_service.py \(~600 LOC\)
- backend/app/config/dogfooding.yaml \(dogfooding configuration\)
- backend/app/middleware/governance.py \(~400 LOC\)
- backend/app/api/routes/governance_mode.py \(~500 LOC\)
- backend/tests/services/governance/test_mode_service.py \(~500 LOC\)

### API Endpoints:
- GET /api/v1/governance/mode
- GET /api/v1/governance/mode/state
- PUT /api/v1/governance/mode
- POST /api/v1/governance/kill-switch
- POST /api/v1/governance/false-positive
- GET /api/v1/governance/metrics
- GET /api/v1/governance/dogfooding/status
- GET /api/v1/governance/health

### Dogfooding Plan:
- Week 1: WARNING mode \(observe, establish baseline\)
- Week 2: SOFT mode \(block critical, warn others\)
- Week 3: FULL mode \(block all violations\)

## Success Criteria:
- Developer friction: <5 min per PR
- First pass rate: >70%
- CEO time saved: >50%

## Total Sprint 108 Progress:
- Day 1: 14 SQLAlchemy models \(~3,168 LOC\) ✅
- Day 2: Auto-Generation Layer \(~3,500 LOC\) ✅
- Day 3: Mode Service + Dogfooding \(~2,000 LOC\) ✅

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
\)")

- Risk-Based Planning Mode \(replaces LOC-based triggers\)
- Brownfield Implementation Strategy \(4-phase approach\)
- Tool-agnostic purity \(100% methodology, zero implementation\)
- Complete migration guide \(5.x → 6.0.0\)

7-Pillar Architecture:
- Pillar 0: Design Thinking Foundation
- Pillar 1: 10-Stage Lifecycle
- Pillar 2: Sprint Planning Governance
- Pillar 3: 4-Tier Classification
- Pillar 4: Quality Gates \(Dual-Track\)
- Pillar 5: SASE Integration
- Pillar 6: Documentation Permanence

Quality Assurance System \(Section 7\):
- Vibecoding Index \(0-100\): 5 weighted signals
- Progressive Routing: Auto-triage by risk level
- Auto-Generation Layer: Intent, Ownership, Context, Attestation
- Kill Switch Criteria: rejection >80%, latency >500ms

SASE Artifacts \(Simplified\):
- AGENTS.md: Industry standard \(60K+ repos\)
- CRP: Consultation-Readiness Proof
- MRP: Merge-Readiness Proof
- VCR: Version Controlled Resolution
- Deprecated: BRS, LPS, MTS

Framework Version: 6.0.0
Release Date: January 29, 2026
Authority: CTO + CPO + CEO Approved
EOF
\)")
